name: Season's Best Thumbprint Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add the egg and vanilla extract to the butter mixture; stir until combined.
3. In a separate bowl, mix the flour, cocoa powder, and salt.
4. Blend the dry ingredients into the butter mixture gradually.
5. Stir in the milk; continue beating until well blended.
6. Fold in the chopped walnuts.
7. Cover the dough and refrigerate for at least 1 hour or until firm enough to handle.
8. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
9. Lightly grease a cookie sheet with butter.
10. Scoop the dough into 1-inch balls and place them on the prepared cookie sheet.
11. Press your thumb into each ball to create a thumbprint design.
12. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until set.
13. Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack.
14. To serve, scoop about 1/2 teaspoon of filling (either vanilla or chocolate cream) into each thumbprint.
15. Top with about 1 teaspoon of pie filling.
16. Store cookies in an airtight container in the refrigerator. Approximately 3 dozen cookies can be made.
